summary: A solid front‑of‑house audience capture of an inspired show – Jerry’s gritty Miracle and a soaring Dark Star shine, though the mix is a bit muffled in the highs.
review: The performance is widely praised as one of the band’s classic “shakedown” nights, highlighted by a blistering rendition of The Miracle and a powerful Dark Star. The recording, made with two Sony 54P mics and an Sennheiser 441, is generally good but lacks bright high‑end detail compared to other audience sources.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (5‑star) and note standout songs, while the recording is judged good but not pristine; the weighted average yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Louder than some other audience tapes but muffled and missing bright highs; high frequencies are subdued, making it harder to separate Phil’s bass from Bobby’s guitar at times.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe the set as a classic Dead “shakedown” with strong momentum, especially in the second half where The Miracle and Dark Star drive the energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His gritty blues‑laden tone on The Miracle and Dark Star is repeatedly praised.
